channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Polkadot - Mumbai Warrior Edition — 62 replies — 182.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - Mumbai Warrior Edition

Open thread • Replies: 62 • Updated: 2026-04-17 22:48 UTC

OP

Hello my fellow PolkaCurry brothers. We are again here, for another thread where we discuss everything related to DOT, Mumbai (the city of DOT), curry and more. The last thread was a resounding success, we managed to keep it alive for literally 41 days. We got paid 11500 + 5*150 = 12250 DOT for the thread and are now living in a period of enlightenment while eating with METAL spoons. Brothers, we have truly reached enlightenment. WE, the Mumbai Warriors will strike yet again and collect generations worth of money !

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

Early replies (first 3)

62115437
We are eating with our METAL spoons in Mumbai. No more hands my brethren. We eat with out spoons, like proud warriors.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62115624
>>62115424
>>62115437

Still gonna larp as an Indian?

62115793
>>62115437

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62118362
How much of this gay token do I need to buy to become a rich chad man in my next life and fuck stacies all day?

62118622
>>62118362
>How much of this gay token do I need to buy to become a rich chad man in my next life and fuck stacies all day?
0. You can already do all of that. If you don't believe me then ask yourself this question "Are there people who became rich without buying DOT?" then ask yourself this question "Do people fuck badies all day long without DOT?".

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62119101
>>62118271
The team needs to move faster

62119758
100$ end of bull run. At the end of the bull run the Mumbai warriors are going to eat with GOLDEN spoons I tell you. Right now we're in the metal era. Sooner or later we'll be at the GOLDEN era. You guys have no clue what's going to happen. We are THE CHAIN. Everyone is going to try to pick up our poops after we finish right into the street. Our poops will be sold on the internet for everyone will know that the Mumbai warriors manure is valuable. Once JAM launches the first use case will be to fully decentralize all the infrastructure of Polkadot, like the website https://polkadot.com/. Sooner or later all the infrastructure associated to Polkadot will be run on-chain. It's the best presentation of it simply working. Why use AWS Amazon if you can simply just host everything directly on DOT and therefore prove to the world that we're capable and ready for the masses?

We shall rise up my Mumbai warriors into a new dawn!

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62121057
>>62119758
Compete against Amazon?

Polkadot better bring any impressive presentation

62121440
>>62121057
>Polkadot better bring any impressive presentation
You do realize that AWS fails multiple times per year and with that it takes down like 50% of the internet? DOT has a 100% uptime. Just simply that one fact makes it superior.

https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2041480013724135847

Having the internet hosted on centralized servers makes no sense. Imagine smart cities hosted on centralized servers and the servers going down. A whole city would simply halt. The reality is that we can't afford down times. We need Web3 cloud to provide a 100% up time. Centralized server simply DO NOT cut it. Every time AWS goes down literally 50% of the internet simply does not work. That's lowkey cooked.

62121668
>>62121440
Great. Name recognition reigns supreme here. Plus Amazon has billions of dollars to address their flaws

62121692
75$ in two years and 5 months

62121723
>>62121668
>Name recognition reigns supreme here
That's great that you pointed that out and at the same time every company sooner or later goes under. There does not exist a company in history of mankind that survived the test of time. No name was ever big enough to sustain itself through time. The only thing that never changes is the fact that everything always changes.

>Plus Amazon has billions of dollars to address their flaws
That's true and the thing is that no amount of money can solve centralized server uptime. There's always something. If you have a system as complex as AWS that's run across multiple countries and relies of tens if not hundreds of millions of lines of code then there's ALWAYS going to be a flaw. It's impossible to have a system as big as AWS, that runs of centralized servers, and that it works all the time. No matter how much money they spent, no matter how many smart engineers they throw at it... The only solution is a Web3 cloud. Can Amazon pivot into a Web3 cloud? Sure. They can literally just use DOT as the underlying infrastructure. DOT is NOT against Amazon. It can compliment it. Amazon can just simply bend the knee and use DOT as their compute provider, right? If Amazon doesn't follow the trend then sooner or later people will abandon it. Why would you use a more expensive cloud provider that has less up time? There is NO point. I as a CS myself know this for a fact. You earlier said
>Name recognition reigns supreme here
To be honest? If I can get a cloud provider for cheaper and have them have more uptime I'd switch in a heartbeat. The same rules don't apply in computers/programming than they do in other sectors. People who are into computers move faster than other sectors, because we're taught BY DEFAULT that things constantly change and never stay the same. If you're paying 5% more and the uptime is 5% less then you're literally shooting yourself in the foot.

62121726
75$ end of race

62121749
>>62121668
What I'm trying to say is that CS is the fastest moving sector for a reason. And the reason is that people in the sector love adopting new technology at a fast pace. If I can get something for 5% cheaper with 5% more uptime and I have the time to migrate my service and it's not too much of a hastle then I'd do it in a heartbeat. Sure if the migration would be EXTREMELY TEDIOUS then I might reconsider. It's just, normally it's not and takes maybe a day or two. Imagine your whole company paying 5% less while your customers would be 5% happier with your service. You'd be stupid not to take that deal, right? Centralized servers ARE EXPENSIVE in nature. Decentralized servers are INEXPENSIVE. Why? Well centralized stuff needs upkeep while decentralized stuff does not. Look at Acurast (https://acurast.com/). They're literally a mobile phone cloud provider. How much does the upkeep for your mobile phone cost? 0$. Do you need janitors and cleaning personnel? Nope. Do you need engineers to run your phone? Nope. Do you see what I mean? The price margin for 1GHz of computing power provided by a mobile device is probably 100x lower than 1GHz of computing power provided by a centralized server. You would switch cloud providers if they offered a 100x lower price while providing a 100% uptime, right? You wouldn't be like "Oh, but their NAME is not AMAZON. OMG OMG. Let's not use this because they have no NAME RECOGNITION. I'd rather pay 100x more and have less uptime than switch. OMG OMG OMG". Do you notice how that sounds once I put it into perspective? Sounds awkward, right?

>>62121692
>>62121726
Soon we shall catapult into the heavens of nirvana.

62121769
>>62121723
> every company goes under
Not quite true. Sure majority do go under, but successful companies adapt to economic changes, merge, or subsidize
Amazon created quite the monopoly in the retail sector. Hard to envision a downturn anytime soon.

>AWS pivots to Web3 cloud
Point taken. There again will DOT have a successful launch once all its compon8 are built in? Will it have enough marketing? Will a new challenger approach?

62121787
>>62121749
Most people would agree Linux is the superior OS against Windows OS

Who still owns the OS space?

Exactly

62121817
>>62121787
>Most people would agree Linux is the superior OS against Windows OS
Great that you point that out. That's an apples to oranges comparison. Linux isn't meant for the average user. It's meant for programmers in a lot of ways. We're also not talking about OS. Are we?

>>62121769
>Not quite true. Sure majority do go under, but successful companies adapt to economic changes, merge, or subsidize
True, and no company survives in it's original form. Everyone adapts to the time. And the time is shifting towards a Web3 cloud.

>Hard to envision a downturn anytime soon.
True, and I'm not saying that. What I'm saying is that Web3 will, over time, take a large share away from it, due to it being cheaper and having more uptime. Again, centralized servers CAN NOT compete with decentralized ones on uptime and cost. And those two factors are literally the only two important factors when it comes to a cloud provider. It has to be cheap and have the largest possible uptime.

>Point taken. There again will DOT have a successful launch once all its compon8 are built in? Will it have enough marketing? Will a new challenger approach?
The thing is that Amazon will just use Web3 clouds. I doubt they'll build their own because they'd have to spent a lot of money to re-invent the wheel. DOT is already ready in a lot of ways. This year JAM hits and that'll transform it into a TRUE Web3 cloud capable of handling the load of AWS. Look, I'm not saying that DOT will IMMEDIATELY kill everyone. It'll be a slow transition and more and more companies will be switching. You know the 20/80 rule? It takes as much time to go from 0% to 20% than it does from 20% to 80%. This was true for smart phones, the internet, cars, ... So, if it took 10 years to go from 0% users to 20% of the population using something then it'll take that same amount of time to go from 20% to 80%. TO BE CONTINUED

62121830
>>62121769
Copied from Google AI

As of early 2026, over 650 million to nearly 1 billion people worldwide are estimated to own or use cryptocurrency. Growth is driven by high adoption in regions like Asia and Africa, with about 6.8% of the global population holding digital assets. While ownership is high, active monthly users are estimated lower, around 30–60 million, indicating a large population of passive holders.

Projected Growth: The number of users is expected to reach 993 million by 2026, with user penetration expected to reach 12.63% of the population, according to 1.
Regional Leaders: Asia dominates with 43% of global users, followed by North America (17%) and Europe (15%).
Top Adoption Countries: India, Nigeria, and Indonesia show high adoption rates, often driven by local currency instability, according to 4 and 3.
User Demographics: Approximately 60% of crypto users are under the age of 34, with a majority being male.

Active on-chain usage continues to rise as mobile wallet installations grow, with over 1.1 billion installations tracked as of early 2026.


We're almost at the 20% mark. BTC was created 17 years ago. So, we'll be at the 20% mark by like 2028 to 2030. So like 19 to 21 years. So, by the year 2050 80% of the world population will be using Web3.

62121862
I'm not sure if you guys are looking at the chart but we're really close to breaking out from the bottom
Look at picrel >>62118020 and >>62118038

62121888
>>62121769
Will it have enough marketing?
No worries my brother. I have made a marketing contract with Jeny Smith (https://thothub.mx/videos/1505023/jeny-smith-polka-dot-pantyhose/). She will market DOT for us. We are set my Mumbai brother

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62121922
>>62121862
I'm not sure if you guys noticed. It's an adam and eve pattern on the 4h chart. Look at picrel

62121953
>>62121862
>>62121922

62122759
>>62121922
>>62121953

Chart looks good

But in the last thread weren't you shorting DOT

62123242
>>62122759
Panjali, be quite you fool (https://boards.4chan.org/biz/thread/61932834#p62069282). Do you want the Mumbai warriors to eat with their hands again? Or do you want to upgrade from our metal spoons to silver and then golden spoons? We, the Mumbai warriors, are painting the graph bullish in order to lure people in, then we strike with a x125 short. We actually been holding short open since 55$. The whole of Mumbai will eat from that singular short. Panjali, you have much to learn about the Vishnu ways. It's ok my son. I forgive you. Together we are strong, divided we are weak.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62124594
>>62123242
Way to promote a project then rug people

62125019
Didint read thread, i Will let My id decide if i buy some DOT when i get home

62125037
>>62125019
w4, E3, Wasb, and purple colour... Yes.. i see...

Not buying.

62125463
this is the most demoralizing dot thread

62126538
>>62124594
>>62125019
>>62125037
>>62125463
Good job my fellow warriors of Mumbai. Thank you for keeping the thread alive with your comments.

>this is the most demoralizing dot thread
How so my Mumbai brother?

>Way to promote a project then rug people
Yes Ganesha. We play the peoples hearts and then we stab them in their hearts.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62126677
Sol 8>297 Is 37X
Dot 1,15 x37= 42

62126931
>>62126538
Holy shit. Are you actually an autistic retard?

62126975
>>62126931
Kamal, are you getting upset again because your mother took away your metal spoon and ate with it? There is plenty of metal spoons for everyone my young warrior of Mumbai.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62127244
>>62126677
Vrishank, that is really good mathematics. Education is really paying off I see. Very good. You might even become a doctor or a lawyer. Yes, you shall reach to the heavens of nirvana one day my young Vrishank. I, Vishnu Kumar, believe in you and so should you my young Vishnu warrior of the mind chain, my young Mumbai warrior, my young metal spoon owner. Soon, we will have silver spoons for everyone, then gold spoon and after that we reach the nirvana of diamond spoons. The whole of Mumbai will eat.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

item.title: You DID secure your moon bag…right, anon? — 35 replies — 140.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
You DID secure your moon bag…right, anon?

Open thread • Replies: 35 • Updated: 2026-04-17 23:05 UTC

OP

You DID secure your moon bag…right, anon?

Early replies (first 3)

62125227
>>62124756
they will get turned away, even if the crowd lined up at the current levels! there will only be 21 million coins and many people have more than one already. the crowds will be turned away since there is not enough of them left. i read that there are already something like 200k bitcoin holders and only a million coins left to be mined. even if each of those new coins went to a new holder (they won't) the maximum number of people that can have a bitcoin at the same time is 1.2 million.

62125283
I placed an order last weekend and initiated an ACH transfer to buy more tomorrow.
t. lucky retard

62125298
>>62125283
>lucky retard
well, half of that statement is true.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62125410
>>62124756
i did buyed

62125446
>>62125310
Yes

62125582
>>62124756
It’s hilarious how dumb people are to think BTC is even worth the 20x considering btc’s product. BTC’s original use was decentralized anonymous currency and it’s become neither decentralized nor anonymous. It’s defied the very thing it set out to do while there are better coins working to become more centralized and other coins that are more anonymous. BTC only caught up once it became more centralized. You want massive banks and governments backing your crypto money laundering scheme and if you know one better than BTC, I would invest in that before BTC because it’s bound to have better margins. Bitcoin has only become a hedge for better, more lucrative coins, ironically enough.

62125583
>>62125227
>21 million coins
>21,000,000 x 100,000,000 satoshi
>2.1 quadrillion
Oh no

62125597
>>62125298
thank you

62125644
>>62124756
If that many normal people are buying bitcoin at £1M it means currency has hyper inflated so much that thats cheap and poorfags can afford it and not only that every poorfag has £1M for bitcorn something has gone wrong, Also what if the new Chinese order decide to just turn it off by decrypting the entire network and dumping the private keys for lols

62125672
>>62125310
It won't dump until July.

62125766
>>62125310
Selling is for fags. Just buy weekly and hold until it hits a million. If it fails (it clearly won’t) then oh well. You lost a gamble.

62125827
>>62125582

>it’s not decentralized
There are 70,000+ nodes worldwide enforcing bitcoins rules. Unless you think 1 guy secretly owns them all?

>its not anonymous
Good luck getting widespread adoption and ETFs for your monero

>it succeeded because it became more centralized
You have this backwards.

I wish you luck with your altcoins but your post is incoherent. It’s bad because it became more centralized while other coins are good for becoming more centralized?

62126111
>>62125583
you think more than 1.2 million people can own a bitcoin at the same time?

the supply is capped, there is simply not enough of them for the crowd. maybe they will be able to rent one for a time but by and large the people that are going to have a bitcoin already do.

62126137
>people like OP turned bullish at the pico top of the bear flag

yikes

62126166
>>62126137
Zoom out nigger. We aren’t selling til $5m

62126217
>>62124756
I picked xrp because $1 -> $1 million is a million x whereas $100k -> $1 million is only 10x

62126224
>had my buy orders at $63k
>dip barely clears $65k
Fuck I'm pretty sure I didn't miss the boat but this price action is demoralizing.

62126314
Why people would rush to buy Bitcoin at 1 mi when they didn't at 100k?

62126338
>>62126314
kek

if we even reach 200K next bullrun consider yourself very lucky

62126414
MY STRATEGIC BITCOIN RESERVE NOW HOLDS 0.22997 BTC!!!!!!

62126672
>>62126314
Protip, people are fucking retarded.

62126806
>>62126111
They can own Bitcoin, they may not own A bitcoin. It's only relevant if you're a math retard.

62126809
>>62126672
>>62126314
Why did people rush to buy Bitcoin at $10k when they didn't at $100?

62126824
i will never understand contemporary bitcoin shit
there are only 21,000,000 bitcoins in existence right?
if bitcoin hit $1,000,000 that would mean the bitcoin market cap would be $21 trillion
compare that to market caps of other industries rn, i don't see how it's physically possible

it seems like people desperately want to believe they can just fucking do what people did with bitcoin in the early 2010s and make a ridiculous amount of money just by uhhh... buying it again and waiting for it to happen again
stupid though

62126828
correct me if i'm wrong about any of that, i want to understand

62126833
is it all a joke?

62126984
>>62125310
I'm thinking of selling but I moht wait a little longer. This pump certainly won't last long though, so getting the timing right will be tough.

62126986
>>62125582
Even if all of this was truth, who cares? It can still make me money.

62126990
>>62126986
wtf is that supposed to mean
i mean, anything CAN make you money

62127238
>>62126824
Idk man it might still hit 300k at least which will hopefully gigapump my alts.

62127252
>>62127238
fair enough i guess

62127263
>>62126314
at $1m they will need it

62127315
>>62124756

item.title: I have been fired lol — 31 replies — 124.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I have been fired lol

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-04-17 23:06 UTC

OP

After 10 years working in a big corpo. I went to my office today and the security guard was there. He didn't let me touch anything and escorted me to HHRR but I already knew what was going to happen.
They didn't give me any reasons. My boss didnt even look at my eyes. They don't want to go to court so they paid me a HUGE severance pay + TWO years of full unemployment benefits (EU country)
And now i'm just here sitting in my living room still processing that after a decade I won't need to wake up early and fight with my bosses and customers anymore. It feels weird.
I probably will take a sabbatical in SEA or something.
Holy shit. It really feels weird.
What would you do /biz/? I honestly dont care about a big gap on my resume and money is not an issue now

Early replies (first 3)

62125619
>>62125608
lol

62125630
What are you good at? Capitalize on your hobbies with seed money from the severance package.

62125698
>>62125630
>Capitalize on your hobbies
>Hiking, filming and traveling.
Oh great i'm going to be a youtuber now.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62125743
>>62125608
Sounds like a country with high taxes. What are the rules for taxes if you're outside of the country? Become a trader, open a business, etc. Get into DeFi if your country is trying to screw you on taxes.

62125760
>>62125698
I like that, in death, all this kike cared about was getting a swipe in at another jewtube kike. What a fuckin loser lmao

62125804
>>62125608
Where's the party, anon?

62125813
>>62125608
>HUGE severance pay + TWO years of full unemployment benefits
i'm jealous

62125875
>>62125743
>Sounds like a country with high taxes
If I legally sell my BTC bag these bastards will take 23%, so yes. But no trading for me. I'm terrible at it.
>>62125804
Pattaya? Im still deciding. But today im going to eat a good steak and get drunk at home.
>>62125813
>i'm jealous
Don't be. It took a DECADE of corporate hell to get this, and piss off a regional CEO enough to not even want to go to court with their army of lawyers. They just wanted me OUT as soon as possible.
Damn, what the fuck did i do lmao.

62125887
>>62125875
1>5>2>4>3

62125891
it’s crazy how bloodless it all is. and now with so many remote or hybrid workers they’ll just schedule a Teams meeting and fire you while you’re at home. you ship back your computer and that’s it. I work for a big US company that has done a lot of rounds of layoffs over the past 6 years. luckily my department was only affected by one of them. my boss’s boss DMd me asking who the two weakest performers were. I gave him two names and they were gone the next day. I think each got 6 months of pay. we just had another round of layoffs last week and I was lucky to survive again. by I’m constantly waiting for it to happen because they’re already in the process of outsourcing most of our work to South America. eventually I won’t be needed.

62125902
Don't get too comfy, you should start finding out what to do now instead of waiting until you need to start looking for a job because you wont find one. I bet if you tried to find a job starting now you would finally get hired 9 months from now.

62125917
>>62125875
5>2>1>3>4

62125920
>>62125608
I sold a business and had some time before getting a job. I was depressed when unemployed.

62125938
>>62125875
Can you take a BTC loan and pay zero taxes on it? Then you can invest the money in a business or something and pay back the loan. There are services that will auto-payback the loan in case BTC goes down so that way you don't get liquidated.

62125952
>>62125875
2>5>3>1>4

62125958
>>62125902
>Don't get too comfy, you should start finding out what to do
You know, after a decade of corporate hell (and before that, years of office wagecucking) is really that bad that I want to take a sabbatical year? I'm 38 so I know that it's a bad idea, but my parents are starting to be really old and maybe this is the last opportunity to do it before I have to take care of them...
also i'm single since Christmas
>>62125938
Are you trying to sell me some shady loan website or something? Anon I don't need risky investments anymore, I have enough money for years and my btc bag won't leave my trezor ever

62126015
>>62125958
Just take some time off, man. No one's going to bat an eye at a one-year gap on your resume, sabbaticals are perfectly acceptable in Europe and you're still young enough to have fun.

62126016
>>62125958
No DESU I'm just literally in your exact situation however I want number to continue to go up instead of number go down. Safe APY is good, BTC is good, trading whatever you feel safe trading is good, and starting businesses is good.

62126026
>>62125608
Plenty of time for investment research. 7 months after I first began looking around I saw a crypto recommendation for a new chain. Then October 2023 happened and everything dipped. It came back from that dip and by 2025 it had become a 10000x. Sure not every year has that kind of opportunity but the point is that it's good to be looking around even if you're not staring at charts of "in the trenches" 24/7.
>SEA
Wherever you go from here but especially there, be sure to look up the history of natural disasters and be prepared for them so you don't end up having to pay even more money due to being unprepared. Pic-related, you never know what's possible. Also, get into growing your own food and having a back-up home. This isn't about an apocalypse, but being prepared for temporary but harsh scenarios. I have a pal who plays games for hours upon hours per week yet still gets more out of his greenhouse than he can eat by himself, so don't shy away due to the initial time investment. Many people throughout history who were well off enough to not have to work, were suddenly hit by economic/environmental disasters and really felt it due to being unprepared. But maybe according to your research and where you live, no such things will happen in your lifetime. The important thing is to look into it.

62126029
>>62125608
Happened to me. I got another job within 3 weeks and got to choose my favorite. 15 years experience C/C++ is pretty employable turns out. Thinking about quitting anyway to go fly airplanes as a second career instead.

62126079
>>62125875


2>5>1>3>4

62126106
>>62125608
That must feel alright. My work time seesaws between 4 months of nonstop all day work and about 4 months off and it feels good to not work for 4 months.

62126110
>>62125608
>losing your job
good
>losing your house
good
>wife leaves you
good
>getting cancer
good

all of these things are good. it will make you stronger. you will come back stronger. I unironically envy you, wish I would lose something important to feed my drive.

62126116
>>62126029
>halo
>fly planes
Reminds me of Joe

62126134
>>62126015
> Europe
Yeah I was about to say, if OP was in burgerland, even a 1 week sabbatical will absolutely destroy your resume and make you permanently blacklisted by ANY hiring company in this abysmal job market.

62126253
>>62126110
Gee thanks siddharta

62126254
>>62126134
Just set your LinkedIn Employment Status to CTO of Stealth Startup. HR still hasn't figured out that means you were actually bug chasing in SEA for a year.

62126303
>>62125608
>2 years pay for getting canned
You better make the most of it you lucky bastard.
>>62125875
I don’t care what you say, I’m still jealous. I know I wouldn’t receive such a nice payout if I was fired. If you don’t at least spend a nice year doing everything you put off, you’ll regret it. Fuck.
-t. wageslave amerifag

62126319
>>62125608
>I probably will take a sabbatical in SEA or something.
GAAYYYYYYYYYYYY
do something more interesting with your life/time holy fuck

nice job though anon
>Holy shit. It really feels weird.
>What would you do /biz/? I honestly dont care about a big gap on my resume and money is not an issue now
depends on other factors

62127314
>>62125875
>>62125887
>>62125917
>>62125952
>>62126079
#4 for me. 4,1,5,2,3 if ranked

62127325
>>62125608
not your personal blog. buy an ad.

item.title: KABOOOOOOM! The Ripple CEO has been working at Ripple for 11 years. — 22 replies — 88.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
KABOOOOOOM! The Ripple CEO has been working at Ripple for 11 years.

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-04-17 22:55 UTC

OP

This is a **MASSIVE** leap for the XRP and crypto community as a whole.

Early replies (first 3)

62117086
>>62116929
Bullish

62117358
Brad is such a chad, he'll take pics no matter how embarrassing it is. Bullish on xrp bros.

62117434

62124543

THIS PROCESS SHOULD GO VERY QUICKLY IN THAT MOST OF THE POINTS ARE ALREADY NEGOTIATED. THANK YOU FOR YOUR ATTENTION TO THIS MATTER! PRESIDENT DONALD J.TRUMP

62124549
>>62124455

62124558

AHAHAHAHAHA ZIGGERNIGGERS COMPLETELY BTFO

>bought the top of silver

bros I might be cursed

62124784
>>62124572
its a blessing. just inverse yourself in the future. im not even joking.

62124856
>>62124455
Yet spot prices are at $120+ and the oil supply is fucked for the rest of the year

Don't attempt to trade this shit unless you want to get rekt. The paper market for oil does its own thing and moves contrary to the market for physical oil. The price of futures is a function of massive hedging by trading firms and oil producers themselves as well as fuckery from the US government.

62127065
>>62124856
>Don't attempt to trade this shit unless you want to get rekt. The paper market for oil does its own thing and moves contrary to the market for physical oil.
Yeah I learned this the hard way the past few weeks. I didn't invest too much into commodities though, less than 5%.

62127188
>>62124856
No shit Sherlock. Do you not realize that futures are supposed to be hedges for oil companies?

62127216
>>62124856
have you tried being smarter?

62127217
>>62124455
only urals price in st.pethesburg is related

62127296
There are no oil baggies, retard

62127320
>>62127296

62127323
>>62127320

62126135
We have 2 options:
a) Bear market is over: That would make it the least brutal bear market in history.
b) Bear market is not over and we're going under 65k.

The bullrun was kinda lame so it wouldn't surprise me if 65k was really the botto.

62127309
>>62126135
>Bear market is over: That would make it the least brutal bear market in history.
Not really, past bear market lows have been around the second to last bull market highs, so the low was supposed to be at 60-70k.

62126890
>>62126711

NOOOOOOOOOOOO

WHY DID YOU DO IT?
IT'S GONNA CRASH NOW

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62126929
I don't want to make a new thread so I'll put it here.

Can someone link me a video on an introduction to trading? I get the basic ideas, but when I open traderview I don't understand anything.
Bullish? Long? Perp bias? idfk

62126944
>>62126929
https://www.youtube.com/@TRLCTradingConfluence/featured

62126945
>>62126929
suck my dick nigger

62126951
>>62126929
what do you want to achieve? do you want to make money or do you want to gamble on shitcoins?

62126953
>>62126951
Yes.

62126967
>>62126953
those are mutually exclusive goals you cannot have both

62127017
>>62126967
I realized these graphs follow certain patterns, so I thought I could write a bot that gambles for me. Then I realized someone else probably already wrote that bot and I can copy it's trades.
But first I need to know how to trade in the first place and I don't know where to start. I hardly understand the graph.

62127055
>>62127017
ur about 50 years too late to the party xd

algorithmic trading is a thing, and you could try your hand at making one, but yeah you need to understand trading in the first place
just watch some youtube tutorials and go from there, ideally tutorials from before 2020 for higher quality

62127057
u shouldn't think of it as anything more than a hobby, and chances are you'll end up wasting a bunch of time on it
don't expect to do anything life-changing

62127069
>>62126919
>using the antinodes of non-linear waves to predict price action
ngmi

62127080
>>62127069
I can see it... I can see tomorrow's prices... I can feel the WAVES

62127081
>>62126711
>BACK IN
You mean, you sold before? So, let me understand here: you sold when it was low, and bought it back now that it's high? Did I get everything right?

62127096
>>62127069
>>62127080
i gotta get fuckin fibonacci pilled and start trading using sacred geometry while snorting adderall

62127115
>>62126711
me too
I just couldn't stand it if it mooned and I was sidelined.

62127215
>>62127115
>>62126711
Yall need to have conviction.
If you want to wait and buy at the bottom don't FOMO when it randomly pumps.
Or if you think it should be pumping, don't ever sell at a loss.

Make a plan and stick to it

62127233
I'm going to make so much money over my margin'd silver miners bros.

I can't believe I'm going to make it..

item.title: The strait is still closed btw. Sending the US navy to block it up even harder only makes things worse This pump is comp… — 43 replies — 31.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
The strait is still closed btw. Sending the US navy to block it up even harder only makes things worse This pump is comp…

Open thread • Replies: 43 • Updated: 2026-04-17 21:45 UTC

OP

The strait is still closed btw. Sending the US navy to block it up even harder only makes things worse

This pump is completely artificial and designed to trap as many plebs as humanely possible

Early replies (first 3)

62120115
open the fuckin strait

62120217
>>62120101
10.000 kbbls/d oil is missing, fertilizer even worse. People going to start walking somewhere if they are hungry and no relive in sight.

62120280
>>62120101
Why not just nuke Iran and move on? Nobody likes them anyways.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62121556
>>62121548
bbls = barrels
k = 1000x
d = day

62121575
>>62121556
Copy that.

62122036
>>62121556
bbls = blue barrels

Standard Oil was the biggest game in town for a long time, and their oil barrels were painted blue, and rapidly became the standard barrel size in the industry. Oil barrels of that size became known as "blue barrels" whether they were actual blue-painted Standard Oil barrels or not.

62122395
>>62121326

62122517
Brer Trump punched the Tar Baby. Comical shit.

62122624
>>62120291
>i know basic news everyone knows
>i know less than investment banks, hedge funds, governments

Banks and NPCs: famous for being correct about everything and never being blinded by greed.

62122745
>>62120101
Bear gf

62122816
>>62122745
Bear wife

62122824
>>62120101
well my option expires in less than 24 hours so it won't matter

62122860
i wish a hot girl would hug me

62122901
bobina is a fucking WHORE

62122909
>>62120291
>>62122624
you write like women

62122948
>>62120101
I'm always astounded by how beloved Bobina is by this board but that Mumette, Crabitha, and Swanica are all rarely used comparatively.

62122959
Yeah but yknow, if I sold right now, I'd win.

62123008
>>62121505
>>62121508
>>62121520
what in tarnation
i still don't understand what happened with the previous short squeeze a few years ago, or what a short squeeze is

62123162
>>62122948
its literally one guy posting it, so he can subtly shill his coin.

62123172
>>62120101
i would pay $2m to be bobo in this pic

62123206
>>62120101
>humanely possible
>humanely
>human

62123257
>>62123008
>someone sees something is poised to do badly, think "this things fucked 100%", bets on it doing badly by going to somebody with shares, telling them to sell today and give them the money from the sale, and in X days they will come back and give them the exact amount of shares they had sold for them (you want them to be cheaper to buy back so you make money)
>The thing in question potentially doesn't get fucked, and infact goes higher than the price it was sold at for them
>Puts the short seller in a shitty position to where they either have to put more money up to close their short sale via either closing and buying the shares at the higher price, or having to put up more collateral while paying interest to keep the short open in the hopes that it swings back in their favor (what that previous short squeeze you eluded to continues to do and continues to obfuscate)
>Is very bad when the short sale in question is done in excess of both the short sellers funds (leverage) and the amount of shares that are available (float) / exist (outstanding shares)
>worse still if/when the entity facilitating this transaction calls you and says "give me my fucking money" (margin call), because they themselves are broke because multiple other entities are doing the exact same thing to them and theyre technically liable for moving "the bag" around (and they can be margin called by their lines of credit)
>further compounded by whoever gets sold the shares then turns around and lets somebody else short them off their hands and so on and so forth ad infimum
>sometimes there aren't even any shares to begin with and they sell off phantom shares that you think you own and are real but are just complete bullshit because the person selling them to you couldn't find it / were unable to borrow it
>This happens all day everyday across every stock and every market while the dtcc (the central counterparty that has to pay everybody out) does absolutely fuck all
Hope this helps!

62123339
>>62123008
When you go short, you MUST buy at some point to close the position.
When you short a stonk, you're borrowing the stonk from someone else and immediately selling at current price. You have to re-buy that stonk at some point and return it to the lender (to close the position).
When you open a short position in futures, you are obligating yourself to sell something for <x> dollars a pop. You need to have the thing in the first place in order to sell it (duh). So if you don't have it to sell, you'd have to buy it in order to fulfill your obligation to sell.
You might not think of it that way, but that is what's happening, mechanically.
Ok.
A short squeeze is when the market looks at something that's massively shorted. The market sees this and says, "If we push price high enough, those shorts will all be stopped out and forced to BUY at a loss to close their position."

When BYND rallied last year, part of the motive was that there were so many shorts on BYND that "if we can hit their stop losses, it will trigger a colossal series of market buy orders."
When silver rallied earlier this year, part of the motive was that "Comex has shorted more silver than it can afford to sell IF price were high enough."
When GME happened so many years ago, part of the motive was "GME is massively shorted, and we want to hit their stops."

So America has been shorting oil to keep price down. Tamping it, as /pmg/ faggots say. This comes with a caveat: eventually America will have to execute all these short contracts. And if oil price is up bigly, oil will be short squeezed.
Hope this make some sense at all.

62123352
>>62123257
>>62123339
ty, ty

62123353
>>62123008
>>62123339
In short (HA HA), a short squeeze is a stop hunt directed at short positions.

62123357
Bobo, where my hug?

62123361
>>62120280
Why not just nuke Israel and move on? Nobody likes them anyways.

62123369
>>62120115
We don't need the strait

62123378
>>62123206
Bearbinaly?
>>62122901
She is not. She is a loyal wife and mother. She is known for teasing tho

62124116
>>62120101
I dont care.

62124225
>>62120280
This is one of the Iranian missile bases that exercise fire control over the strait of Hormuz. It's under 200-400 meters of hard rock. Try nuking it and see how it goes.

62126020
>>62124225
No need to nuke it, the US blew it up a month ago.

62127029
>>62126020
Blew up the entrance, which gets cleared in a day

item.title: I am just over halfway through a week long fast — 12 replies — 23.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
I am just over halfway through a week long fast

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-04-17 22:37 UTC

OP

If I don't make it, LINK will never pump. Passed the hardest day at least, day 3. However my girlfriend is having her friends over this evening for take away, which will be my next challenge.

Early replies (first 3)

62124722
supremely based. why have you decided to fast?

62124768
>>62124641
im ending my 3 day fast tomorrow evening

62124777
your gf is aware of this and having her friends over?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62124919
>>62124722
Thanks bro. I've fasted in the past, lots of OMAD, some shorter 2-3 day fasts, my longest and latest being 5.5 days, so wanted to hit a week.

I always feel great after them and I had let shit go a little bit. Straight into slight caloric deficit after and I'm hitting the gym 5x times a week now, so just kick-starting with this and taking back control.

>>62124768
Fuarrkk let's go brah, let's pump link through fasting

>>62124777
Wasted trips, it was organized way before I personally decided to fast.

62125032
>>62124919
i've done 3 day fasts, a few times. will 7 days be much harder?

62125123
>>62125032
I mean, I still have just over 3 days to go, and yes there's been hard times, especially end of day 3 for me.
Today feels way easier though, just gotta stay on top of electrolytes and distract yourself from thinking of food.
Hunger pants have mostly gone at this point thankfully.

I remember at the end of the 5.5 day one thinking I could have kept going, but my goal had been 5 days.
It's not easy, but it's a trip

62125129
>>62125123
*Pangs lol

Should lost about 2kg of body fat by the end.
And with water weight about 6-7kg (that comes back as you refeed)

62126248
I dropped down to 92kg from 115kg since August last year. Was pretty easy to start with just cutting out chocolates/snacks/crisps and only having fast food occasionally but now it's slowed down.
Feeling better but aiming at 80kg and around 15% bodyfat, so started hitting the gym and tracking my calories for a 500kcal deficit, wish me luck bros.
Tired of being a fat lethargic slob

62126722
>>62126248
Nice dude. I'm sitting around 88kg, although hopefully that like 85.5/86 by Monday.
I'm the same, now counting calories, and hitting the gym. Trying to eat delicious food too, it's possible with planning.

WAGMI

62127141
drink water or full fast?

62127185
>>62124641
>I want to not eat
>better get an app!

hellworld linkies are even more cringe than I imagined

I'm just dropping in from the timeline where the chosen linkies made it in late 2019. All the loser linkies got droned by the old-style governments during their celebration "yacht party"

62127211
>>62127141
Water and electrolytes

item.title: How much BTC you holding right now? — 76 replies — 22.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
How much BTC you holding right now?

Open thread • Replies: 76 • Updated: 2026-04-17 19:46 UTC

OP

How much BTC do you currently have in your wallet, and what’s your target amount to hold before it reaches $1M?

>I mean, if you don’t even have at least 1 BTC and you’re in crypto, it still feels pointless.

Early replies (first 3)

62114988
>>62114930
1.03

ill keep buying as pay cheques come in as long as we stay around the 4 year SMA

ill stop buying when we start running up and instead start selling to buy other assets, probably pay off my mortgage

62115186
>>62114930
i cope with my 5 stack and tell myself in 10 years i'll make it.

62115188
>>62114930
350 BTC or so.

Feels good. I'm living pretty modestly in a $2MM 5000 square foot house in a low cost state. Probably going to build a home gym extension with a sauna and hyperbaric oxygen chamber. Also a small fallout shelter just to be comfy.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62121409
>>62114930
Only 1. It's not enough sadly

62121442
>>62119498
A bitcoin ETF on the American stock market.

62121496
>>62114930
I have 1 BTC spot and like 3 or 4 worth in ETFs in my IRA. I don't buy spot BTC at all and buy alts instead because I'm a masochist and want to feel like kys'ing myself everyday until I finally giga/makeit/ in altszn. I will probably hodl my 1 bitshit forever and sell everything else in the end.

62122124
>>62117820
Would be great to see TBVs go live soon. Could really open up more BTC use cases beyond just holding.

62122129
>>62115773
It's kinda enough , you can increase little by little.

62122136
>>62116578
tomorrow, don't worry about it lol

62122141
People are still buying the Epstein Pedo coin? Oof, brutal

62123324
>>62116632
Yeah you can

62123590
>>62122124
I'm not convinced of bitcoin usecases. It's just another promising word spoken

62123601
>>62121442
That's centralized shit

62123609
>>62122141
Hahahaa ngmi

62123784
>>62120210
It’s part of a BTCFi concept around using BTC in DeFi without changing custody assumptions.

62123785
>>62116881
Guess it's about hardwork

62123812
>>62115489
I'll retire early with that money

62123814
>>62123784
Why do you like risking when you can hold only

62123856
>>62115773
Not enough

62123911
>>62121496
Just sell it today bobo

62124101
>>62114930
I hold 0.84 btc at Kraken

62124449
>>62123590
You have a point here tho. The use cases are still early, so skepticism makes sense. What’s interesting is the idea of extending utility while keeping Bitcoin unchanged and kept in self custody.

62124459
>>62116911
>ETH is cool but why LINK?

62124487
2.3 BTC and 36 ETH.

62124590
>>62115225
Giga Chad, I kneel

62124643
>>62117007
His choice lol

62125521
>>62117185
$30k? Let's see if you're right.

62125560
1.4 BTC

It doesn't feel like enough. I'm 36 now, and my dream of living a wealthy and comfy life before the age of 40 is basically done for. Is it even possible to have a fun life at 50+? I really lament not getting into BTC back in 2014. It's not like I didn't know about it back then.

62126507
Is BTC even worth having anymore? Seems to have had pretty shit returns since the covid boom.

62126533
>>62114930
https://timesofmalta.com/article/elusive-bitcoin-founder-adam-back-lives-malta.1126648
What are the financial implications of this?

62126561
>>62123814
What risk are you talking about here? Also, it’s better now that there are returns instead of just holding with no yield. This setup is way better overall.

62126610
>>62117395
but why did you sell?

62126617
>>62117832
buy the bottom? but at which price?
